visual_features.overall_style: Warm boutique-hotel aesthetic with a premium, slightly vintage feel. Combines a rich dark green brand color with gold accents and a cozy interior-photo hero. Style is modern but with classic touches (badge logo, serif wordmark, gold dividers) that evoke a Parisian, refined atmosphere.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: Hierarchy is driven by scale, contrast, and positioning. The full-bleed bedroom hero image forms the backdrop. Centered within it, the large geometric logo badge is the primary focal point, reinforced by the tagline directly above it and thin gold divider lines. The top navigation bar is high-contrast (solid dark green) and visually strong but secondary to the hero. The booking status bar (gold strip) and the booking widget (white controls with a strong dark green 'BOOK' button) form a secondary focal cluster just below the logo, guiding users toward conversion.
visual_features.whitespace_usage: Whitespace is used as visual breathing room around key elements in the hero: ample vertical spacing above and below the logo, comfortable padding in the nav bar, and generous internal padding in the booking bar and fields. Elements do not feel cramped despite the dense hero composition because of consistent spacing and clear separation layers (nav, hero content, booking tools).
unique_elements: Hexagonal/diamond-shaped brand badge with gold outline and centered serif logotype ('LE PETIT COSY HOTEL').
Thin gold horizontal divider lines flanking the hero tagline, adding a refined, classic touch.
Booking widget enclosed in a subtle gold, angular outline echoing the hexagonal logo geometry.
Offset header-logo block in the top-left with a diagonal/angled bottom edge creating a layered, bespoke card-like brand element.
Use of real interior photography (deep blue walls, warm copper lamps) as a key part of the visual identity, reinforcing the 'cosy' boutique character.

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Implicit 12-column desktop grid with a centered content container. Hero elements (logo, tagline, booking widget) are visually centered, while the header uses a full-width bar with logo on the left and navigation on the right.
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Full-width hero layout with an overlaid top navigation bar. Content follows a linear top-to-bottom flow within the hero: header → tagline → logo badge → booking status bar → booking widget.
layout_characteristics.section_organization: Top fixed-like header (logo block + nav + language switch + book CTA) sits on a solid dark green background. Below, a single full-height hero section features the room photo. Within the hero, content is vertically stacked and centered: tagline with dividers, large logo badge, then two stacked functional bars (status bar and booking form). Each bar is horizontally aligned and visually spans the central portion of the screen.
layout_characteristics.alignment: Strong center alignment in the hero for brand and booking elements. Navigation items are right-aligned in the header, logo block left-aligned. Text within bars is horizontally distributed (left to right) but vertically centered within their containers. Overall, the layout adheres to a strict alignment grid for a polished, upscale effect.
responsive_hints: Centralized hero content suggests it will scale down proportionally on smaller viewports.
Booking bar and widget appear as horizontally laid-out form controls likely to stack into multiple rows or a vertical block on mobile.
Top navigation with spaced menu items indicates a breakpoint where it will probably collapse into a hamburger menu to preserve spacing.
Full-bleed hero image is likely using background-position: center / cover to maintain focus on the bed and lamps at different aspect ratios.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: The content in the visible design is arranged in a linear, top-to-bottom sequence within a single hero section. Starting at the top: a full-width dark green header with a left-aligned logo badge card overlapping the hero image, language switch in the center, and horizontal navigation with a rightmost 'BOOK' link/CTA. Below this, in the visual center of the hero, a tagline is horizontally centered with gold divider lines on each side. Directly beneath sits the large hexagonal logo badge. Under the logo, a full-width (within the content column) gold status bar shows hotel availability text. Finally, the main booking widget is positioned over the lower part of the bed image, structured as a horizontal strip of date inputs, guest count selector, and a primary 'BOOK' button, all inside a gold-outline frame.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: Vertically, the header occupies roughly 12–15% of the viewport height, the central logo and tagline cluster around 30–40%, and the booking status + booking widget area about 20–25%, leaving background image space around these elements as visual breathing room. Horizontally, the main content is constrained to a center column occupying about 60–70% of the viewport width, with the hero image extending edge-to-edge. The logo badge and booking elements are given ample padding and margin so they don’t visually collide with the bed image details.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Moderately spacious layout. While multiple functional elements (navigation, tagline, logo, status strip, booking form) coexist within one viewport, each block is separated by clear vertical spacing and internal padding, leading to an overall feeling of controlled density. It neither feels minimalistic nor cluttered, but tuned to look rich and inviting.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: The eye typically follows a Z-pattern. First, it lands on the top-left logo block and phone number, then moves horizontally across to the main navigation and 'BOOK' link. Next, it drops diagonally to the center tagline and large logo badge, which act as the dominant visual anchor. From there, the eye naturally moves downward to the gold status bar and further to the white booking controls and dark green 'BOOK' button. Background lamps on either side subtly frame and recenter focus on the middle column.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Buttons appear as medium-sized rounded rectangles with approximately 4–6px corner radius. Primary buttons (e.g., hero 'BOOK') use a solid dark green background (#0B3F36 / #0C5A47), white uppercase or semi-uppercase text, and no visible border, relying on color contrast for affordance. They sit slightly elevated over the background (subtle drop shadow or simply strong contrast). Secondary buttons/labels (e.g., gold hotel status chip) use the light gold background (#F4D37A) with dark green text and possibly a thin darker boundary, maintaining brand consistency. Padding appears around 10–12px vertically and 20–24px horizontally.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Typically implemented as either <button> elements or <a> tags styled as buttons. The primary CTA could be a <a> inside the nav for global booking, and within the hero, a semantic <button type='submit'> for form submission. Buttons may include an inner <span> for text to facilitate icon+label patterns.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Card-like elements include the top-left logo block and the booking widget container. They feature solid background colors (dark green for logo block, white for booking inputs, gold for status bar) with soft rounded corners (4–8px radius). Shadows are minimal or non-existent; separation is primarily through color contrast and borders. The booking widget has a distinctive gold geometric outline echoing the brand badge, giving it a custom card silhouette without heavy elevation. Internal padding is generous (16–24px), ensuring content doesn’t touch edges.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern:
Likely built as
containers using flexbox for internal layout. The booking widget card would contain grouped form controls: a flex row with date inputs, guest selector, and submit button. The logo card in the header is probably another
anchored to the top-left with an internal or

ui_patterns.visual_description: Form elements (date pickers, guest count) use white rectangular fields with subtle corner rounding (~4px), light gray borders (#E0E0E0), and inner padding around 8–10px. The labels for fields (e.g., 'Adult(s)') are lightly styled text directly above or inline to the left of their respective controls within the same bar. Fields are spaced evenly with consistent gaps between each control. Overall form density is moderate—compact enough for a single-line layout but with enough padding to feel premium rather than utilitarian. Focus states likely use a darker border or subtle glow in brand green.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Forms are expected to use a single <form> element wrapping the booking controls. Internally, flexbox or CSS grid would create a horizontal row of grouped controls: two date inputs, a dropdown or numeric input for guests, and a submit button. Labels could be implemented as <label> elements tied to their inputs via for/id attributes, arranged in stacked or inline label-input pairs inside small wrapper divs.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Top navigation is a horizontal bar on a dark green background. Menu items (ROOMS, RESTAURANT, GUEST BOOK, CONTACT, BOOK) are rendered as uppercase or small-caps sans-serif text, evenly spaced with generous horizontal padding, and likely white or very light text color for contrast. The active or highlighted link (e.g., CONTACT appears slightly brighter) is probably indicated via color change (e.g., gold accent) or subtle underline. The 'BOOK' item at the far right is visually distinct (possibly bolder or with bullet separators on both sides) to emphasize its CTA nature. Language toggles (FR | EN) in the top center use simple small text with a vertical separator.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Navigation likely implemented as a <nav> containing an unordered list (<ul><li><a> pattern). The language switcher may be a separate inline list or a pair of <a> elements. The 'BOOK' nav item might be a special class (e.g., .nav-cta) styled to look bolder and perhaps button-like. On mobile, the structure would lend itself to a collapsible menu triggered by a hamburger button.

ui_patterns.visual_description: The most evident list-like pattern is the inline information row in the gold status bar ("LE PETIT COSY HÔTEL : INDISPONIBLE | OTHER WEBSITES : INDISPONIBLE"). Items are horizontally arranged, with light separators (colon and vertical bar) instead of heavy dividers, and key words like 'INDISPONIBLE' highlighted in bold dark green, creating a subtle emphasis list within a single line. Overall list styling favors text differentiation (bold/regular) and spacing over visual bullets.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: These list items are likely implemented either as inline <span> elements inside a flex container or as a small <ul> with li elements displayed inline or inline-flex. In general content areas, the site would likely use simple <ul>/<ol> lists, optionally styled with custom bullets or dashes and consistent vertical spacing (8–12px) between list items.

heading.visual_style: Headings and hero taglines appear to use a clean, modern sans-serif with fairly even stroke weight and moderate letter spacing, while the logo wordmark is an elegant serif. Overall, the UI headings lean towards a refined, neutral sans-serif that doesn’t compete with the decorative brand logo.

body.visual_style: Body text looks like a humanist sans-serif: readable, slightly rounded, and friendly without being casual. It supports the cozy, approachable tone while staying elegant.

spacing_system.component_padding: Buttons and small chips: 8–12px vertical, 16–24px horizontal (2x–3x base). Cards and bars (status strip, booking widget): 16–24px internal padding on all sides. Nav items: ~16px horizontal padding between link text and edges.
spacing_system.section_margin: Large vertical separations between major blocks (tagline ↔ logo, logo ↔ status bar, status bar ↔ booking widget) appear to use 24–40px spacing, roughly 3x–5x the base unit, creating a tiered vertical rhythm.

interaction_hints: Buttons likely darken or lighten on hover, with a smooth color-transition (150–250ms) to convey clickability without being flashy.
Navigation links probably use a color change to gold, underline, or bottom border on hover/active, matching the brand accent.
Form inputs may show a slightly thicker border in brand green and/or subtle shadow on focus to improve affordance.
Booking widget could use micro-interactions such as date picker popovers and numeric steppers for the guest count, with light fade/slide transitions.
Hero elements (like the logo badge or booking bar) might use gentle fade-in or upward slide animations on page load to emphasize sophistication while keeping motion subtle.
